skill.name: youtube-video-api-skill
skill.description: Extract channel and video metrics from a YouTube channel by calling the BrowserAct YouTube Video API workflow. The skill runs a local Python script, sends the channel URL and ordering mode to BrowserAct, polls for completion, and prints structured results.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube channel and video metrics through the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access and environment variable access are true positives, but they match the declared BrowserAct API workflow. The critical heuristic is dismissed because no hidden exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection was found in the reviewed files. Publication is acceptable with a warning because the skill executes a script, uses a user API key, and sends channel input to a third-party API.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Request Uses User API Key
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a Bearer token to the declared BrowserAct API endpoint. This is required for the skill, but it exposes user credentials to a third-party service if the endpoint or workflow is not trusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior is declared, so the risk is credential exposure to the intended service rather than hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Script Execution With Network Polling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python script that starts a remote workflow and polls for task status. The command is fixed, but users should understand that the skill executes local code and may run for several minutes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The invocation and polling loop are explicit and semantically required for the BrowserAct workflow. I did not find dynamic shell construction or user-controlled command injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Command Markers
low_findings.description: Many external command findings in SKILL.md are caused by Markdown inline code, parameter names, or a fenced invocation example. The reviewed content does not show Ruby backtick execution or dynamic shell evaluation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation text or Markdown formatting, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. The only real command shown is the fixed Python invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match prose and field names related to API usage and video output. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static labels do not correspond to cryptographic code in context. The cited lines describe API inputs and output fields only.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: The skill contains operational instructions for using the BrowserAct API, but no text claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or requests security review bypass.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I reviewed the instruction-heavy sections and found normal skill usage guidance. There is no evidence of override language or review manipulation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Calls to BrowserAct API
dangerous_patterns.description: The script performs POST and GET requests to api.browseract.com with a Bearer token.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct requests calls and a fixed API base URL are visible in the script. The destination matches the declared service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Credential Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment and refuses to run without it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential access is explicit in metadata, documentation, and code. The key is only used for the declared Authorization header in reviewed code.

content.value_statement: Manual YouTube channel research is slow and inconsistent. This skill calls BrowserAct to collect channel details and video metrics from a selected channel.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper script for a BrowserAct YouTube workflow.
Accepts a YouTube channel URL as the main input.
Supports Latest, Popular, and Earliest video ordering modes.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Prints channel fields and video metrics returned by the API.
Detects a missing BrowserAct API key before making requests.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends the channel URL and API token to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and YouTube page behavior.
Does not validate whether a channel URL is public or allowed to process.

use_cases.title: Competitor Channel Monitoring
use_cases.description: Collect recent or popular video metrics from competitor channels for recurring performance reports.

use_cases.title: Creator Portfolio Research
use_cases.description: Review channel size, engagement signals, and top videos before outreach or sponsorship planning.

use_cases.title: Content Strategy Review
use_cases.description: Compare latest, popular, and earliest videos to understand publishing patterns and audience response.

prompt_templates.title: Get Popular Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to extract Popular videos from this YouTube channel: <channel URL>. Summarize the channel metrics and strongest videos.

prompt_templates.title: Track Latest Uploads
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Latest videos for <channel URL>. Highlight publishing cadence, view counts, and any notable engagement changes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Channel Origins
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the skill with Earliest for <channel URL>. Explain how the early content differs from the current channel direction.

prompt_templates.title: Build Competitive Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Popular videos for <channel URL>. Create a competitor brief covering themes, engagement patterns, and content opportunities.

output_examples.input: Popular videos for a public technology channel
output_examples.output: A concise channel summary with subscriber count, top video titles, video links, views, likes, comments, durations, and publish dates.

output_examples.input: Latest videos for a creator channel
output_examples.output: A dated upload list with recent titles, view counts, durations, and notes about posting cadence.

output_examples.input: Earliest videos for a brand channel
output_examples.output: An overview of early channel content with video metrics and observations about initial positioning.

best_practices: Confirm the BrowserAct API key is set before running the script.
Use public channel URLs and avoid sensitive or private research targets.
State the desired ordering mode clearly: Latest, Popular, or Earliest.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into prompts or shared documents.
Do not run repeated jobs without checking BrowserAct rate limits or costs.
Do not treat returned metrics as permanent because YouTube data changes often.

faq.question: What credentials does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment so the Python script can call BrowserAct.

faq.question: Does it scrape YouTube directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The local script starts a BrowserAct workflow and polls BrowserAct for the result.

faq.question: What video ordering modes are supported?
faq.answer: The documented modes are Latest, Popular, and Earliest.

faq.question: Can it analyze private videos?
faq.answer: No private video access is documented. Use it for public channel pages only.

faq.question: Why can the task take several minutes?
faq.answer: BrowserAct runs an automated workflow and the script polls for completion every ten seconds.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it runs a script, uses a secret, and calls a third-party API.
